rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A MUNICIPAL ET AL VS. ANNE KIHAGI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Dale Duncan And Marta Munoz Mendozas Motion For Leave To Intervene In Action (Proposed Complaint In Intervention Attached As Exhibit 1)
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 15, 2022 line 1. NOTICE OF DALE DUNCAN AND MARTA MUNOZ MENDOZA'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO INTERVENE IN ACTION is DENIED. Moving party failed to cite any authority on point allowing an intervention under the circumstances of this case and its procedural posture. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: 6315 THIRD STREET, LLC VS. GREGORY HAYNES ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Compliance Hearing Pursuant To Jun-14-2022 Order Granting Motion For Issue And Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 15, 2022 line 2. COMPLIANCE HEARING PURSUANT TO JUNE 14, 2022 ORDER GRANTING MOTION FOR ISSUE AND MONETARY SANCTIONS is GRANTED in part and Moot in part. Motion is moot (except for sanctions) as to Larry Haynes who sat for a deposition per this Court's June 14, 2022 Order. Motion for issue sanctions is GRANTED as to Gregory Haynes as requested. See Notice of Motion filed on April 6, 2022 page 2 lines 14 to 22. June 14, 2022 Order ordering the deposition of Gregory Haynes is vacated; June 14, 2022 order remains in effect as to all other respects. Plaintiff to prepare a supplemental order reflecting this ruling. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: PHUONG T. NGUYEN VS. LONDON BREED ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Phuong T. Nguyen'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Reconsider Its Ruling On Order Filed From May 27, 2022 Concerning Plaintiff'S Demurrer To Answer And Motion To Strike Answer; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Supporting Declaration
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 15, 2022 line 3. PLAINTIFF PHUONG T. NGUYEN'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO RECONSIDER ITS RULING ON ORDER FILED FROM MAY 27, 2022 CONCERNING PLAINTIFF'S DEMURRER TO ANSWER AND MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER is DENIED. To the extent Plaintiff is seeking a ruling on her request for judicial notice filed in conjunction with the Demurer and the Motion to Strike on April 25, 2022, the request for judicial notice is granted as the register of actions, complaint, answer and CCP 446 have been reviewed and considered by the Court in rendering the decisions on those motions due to the nature and arguments advanced in those motions. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: ALICIA DURAN PITTS VS. J.P. MORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant J.P. Morgan Chase Bank, N.A., The Bank Of New York Mellon Formerly Known As The Demurrer To Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 15, 2022 line 5. DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts addressing statute of limitations and bankruptcy schedule issue. Plaintiff shall further join the new owner of the property and the co-borrower's estate as parties to this action. The Court makes no rulings as to other arguments raised in the demurrer at this time and they may be raised in future motions if necessary at that time. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: KEYHAN MOHANNA, INDIVIDUALLY, AND AS TRUSTEE OF VS. MM CLUB L.L.C. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 15, 2022 line 6. DEFENDANT MM CLUB L.L.C.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer is overruled as to the cause of action for elder abuse which is sufficiently stated. Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the cause of action for cancellation of instruments, which is barred by res judicata. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to the cause of action for fraud/civil conspiracy for Plaintiff to allege facts with requisite particularity to support each element of this cause of action. Plaintiff shall have 10 days from notice of entry of order to file and serve and amend complaint. If an amended complaint is not filed, Defendant shall file an answer to the current complaint (subject to this ruling) within 12 days of notice of entry of order. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
